title: My English Voice: Grade 8 Capstone Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Using few words to say something clearly.
choices: concise
zeal
loathe
rounds.answer: concise
rounds.explanation: concise: Using few words to say something clearly.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To remember and honour someone or something, often with a ceremony.
choices: concise
zeal
commemorate
rounds.answer: commemorate
rounds.explanation: commemorate: To remember and honour someone or something, often with a ceremony.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A very cruel or terrible act
choices: zeal
atrocity
concise
rounds.answer: atrocity
rounds.explanation: atrocity: A very cruel or terrible act

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A structure used to block people or vehicles from passing.
rounds.answer: barricade

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To become smaller, less strong, or less important over time.
rounds.answer: wane

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: making someone believe something that is not true; misleading
rounds.answer: deceptive

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: The protesters built a barricade across the road using old crates.
Eating healthy food can improve your longevity.
She was able to articulate her opinion during the class discussion.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: A natural ability to do something well.
A situation or event that produces a large amount of something good.
Having the ability to achieve things in a clever and often deceptive way.
The force or speed of something moving, especially when it is getting faster.
rounds.answer: A natural ability to do something well.
rounds.explanation: She has a great aptitude for learning new languages quickly.

choices: A person or group that opposes or fights against another.
A situation or event that produces a large amount of something good.
Something that is given up or allowed in order to reach an agreement.
The force or speed of something moving, especially when it is getting faster.
rounds.answer: A person or group that opposes or fights against another.
rounds.explanation: In the competition, each team tried to outsmart their adversary to win.

choices: a word that means the opposite of another word
lacking experience or sophistication; easily fooled or deceived
making someone believe something that is not true; misleading
to hesitate or lose strength when doing something
rounds.answer: to hesitate or lose strength when doing something
rounds.explanation: She began to falter in her speech when she saw the large crowd.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What is the central idea of this text?
choices: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.
Using few words to say something clearly.
A structure used to block people or vehicles from passing.
rounds.answer: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.

choices: Using few words to say something clearly.

Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.
rounds.answer: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.

rounds.prompt: What is the central idea of this text?
choices: A structure used to block people or vehicles from passing.
Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.
Using few words to say something clearly.
rounds.answer: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.target: Explain four portfolio choices and support each choice with precise evidence.
Eating healthy food can improve your longevity.
six audience questions with relevant evidence, source awareness and thoughtful clarification.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The car gained momentum as it rolled down the hill.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The war led to many atrocities that shocked the world.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The teacher's benign smile made the students feel comfortable in class.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'aptitude' mean in this unit?
choices: A natural ability to do something well.
Using few words to say something clearly.
A strong feeling of enthusiasm and eagerness.
rounds.answer: A natural ability to do something well.
rounds.explanation: aptitude: A natural ability to do something well.

rounds.prompt: What does 'desist' mean in this unit?
choices: Using few words to say something clearly.
A strong feeling of enthusiasm and eagerness.
To stop doing something
rounds.answer: To stop doing something
rounds.explanation: desist: To stop doing something

rounds.prompt: What does 'zeal' mean in this unit?
choices: To feel strong dislike or hatred for something or someone.
A strong feeling of enthusiasm and eagerness.
Using few words to say something clearly.
rounds.answer: A strong feeling of enthusiasm and eagerness.
rounds.explanation: zeal: A strong feeling of enthusiasm and eagerness.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

choices: Adjective
Noun
Verb
rounds.answer: Adjective
rounds.explanation: pristine is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is '4. disposition'?
choices: Verb
Adjective
Noun
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: 4. disposition is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

choices: Adjective
Verb
Noun
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: feign is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

pairs: A strong feeling of enthusiasm and eagerness.
To feel strong dislike or hatred for something or someone.
To stop something from happening or developing; to hold back.
A natural ability to do something well.
The force or speed of something moving, especially when it is getting faster.
making someone believe something that is not true; misleading
A temporary break from something difficult or unpleasant.
A strong effort to achieve something important, often related to a cause or belief.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

choices: To block something so that it cannot move or be seen.
To come between two people or groups to help settle a problem or stop something bad.
To feel strong dislike or hatred for something or someone.
To pretend to have something or to be something that you are not.
rounds.answer: To feel strong dislike or hatred for something or someone.
rounds.explanation: I loathe doing homework on weekends when the weather is beautiful.

choices: To express thoughts and ideas clearly and effectively.
To make something happen faster; to hurry.
To remember and honour someone or something, often with a ceremony.
To stop something from happening or developing; to hold back.
rounds.answer: To stop something from happening or developing; to hold back.
rounds.explanation: The teacher tried to stifle the noise in the classroom by asking everyone to be quiet.

choices: To become smaller, less strong, or less important over time.
To express thoughts and ideas clearly and effectively.
To make something happen faster; to hurry.
Verb: to publicly support or recommend. Noun: a person who supports a cause.
rounds.answer: To become smaller, less strong, or less important over time.
rounds.explanation: As the evening went on, the excitement at the party began to wane.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

choices: A statement that explains the meaning of a word.
A strong feeling of enthusiasm and eagerness.
The length of time that a person or thing lives or lasts.
Using few words to say something clearly.
rounds.answer: Using few words to say something clearly.
rounds.explanation: The teacher asked us to write a concise summary of the story.

choices: A statement that explains the meaning of a word.
A structure used to block people or vehicles from passing.
The length of time that a person or thing lives or lasts.
To give or set aside a certain amount for a specific purpose.
rounds.answer: A structure used to block people or vehicles from passing.
rounds.explanation: The protesters built a barricade across the road using old crates.

choices: To come between two people or groups to help settle a problem or stop something bad.
To feel very happy and excited, especially because of a success or victory.
To pretend to have something or to be something that you are not.
To take control of something, especially a position of power, without the right to do so.
rounds.answer: To feel very happy and excited, especially because of a success or victory.
rounds.explanation: After winning the game, the team began to exult and celebrate their victory.
